title: A Chance To Change | HG Amitasana Dasa
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: In the Bhagavad Gita, Krishna mentions that the living entities, the spirit soul, the Atma is his Amsha.
And Prabhupada explains that how as Amsha of the Lord, we are qualitatively same as that of Lord.
Prabhupada gives example of how a drop of water in sea has the same characteristics that of the entire sea or the entire ocean.
A drop of water is very minuscule compared to the entire ocean.
So being Amsha of the Lord, qualitatively we are one with the Lord and we also are Sat, Chit and Ananda.
Sat means eternal, Chit means full of knowledge and Ananda, blissful.
And these Amsha of the Lord in the spiritual world, in the association of the Lord, are blissfully engaged in the service of the Lord.
In the spiritual world, the Lord is the centre. There is no Aham, there is pure love.
And everyone is engaged in serving others.
A devotee may engage in serving other devotees, but keeping Lord in the centre.
So there the mentality is Pehle Aap, Your service.
So there the mentality is Pehle Aap, Your service.
So there the mentality is Pehle Aap, Your service.
your seva enabling others to serve Krishna directly or indirectly everyone
is engaged in serving the Supreme Lord in glorifying the Supreme Lord but that
I'm sure of the Lord when he develops an eacha we have a free will we have a
power to choose Krishna has given us the free will so when that I'm sure desires
eacha what is that eacha desires to be glorified to be served to be the center
of attraction to be famous in other words take the position of the Lord now
to fulfill that desire some Souls cannot be forced to serve that Jeeva who has
developed that desire just like in the material world
When some souls rebel against the established law, when they don't want to abide by the law, when they don't want to go by the overall scheme of things, the way things have been planned,
and if he violates that law, violates that arrangement, if he rebels against the law or against the government, then the government is forced to make a prison house.
Government can always isolate such people who have violated the laws, who are criminals, who have committed crimes.
Who have committed some crime, who have rebelled against the government, who are not willing to abide by the laws.
They can put them in a line and shoot them.
But the government wants to give them an opportunity to reform.
Yes, that mentality has come, criminal mentality has arisen.
A person who is a law abiding citizen has committed a crime.
Now rather than just shooting him, let's give an opportunity to reform him of his criminal mentality.
So what does the government do?
Government invests money, invests energy in building a prison house.
There are some citizens who will break the laws.
In other words, who will commit a crime.
To reform them, a prison house is made.
So likewise the original crime, ichcha, I want to be the lord.
I want to be the lord.
I want to not follow the law
